5 years ago our life looked very different.

Sagar was organising events and we were constantly out weekday parties, weekend parties, late nights that turned into late mornings, and hangovers that sometimes lasted two days ( longer than the actual party itself 😅 )
It was fun, it really was. But somewhere along the way we realised we were waking up tired, feeling unhealthy, fighting more, and honestly… not even remembering half the nights we called “the best nights of our life”.

I finally understood what people mean when they say life changes after marriage. It’s not that you stop having fun. It’s that your idea of fun slowly changes.

Today we still meet friends, still celebrate, still enjoy the occasional night out. But now we also choose slow mornings, work we’re proud of, better health, and a future we’re building together.

Somewhere around turning 30, our bodies also made the decision very clear for us 😄 even the thought of a hangover now feels like YAPPPAAAHHH NAMASKARA 🙏🏻

And now waking up at 7am for yoga by a pond feels more like us than anything else ever did.

Here’s to new versions of ourselves… and choosing sunrise over hangovers (most days) 😉

📍 @coorgmarriottresortandspa

My mom banned me from the kitchen… so I did THIS 😭
So obviously… certified jugaadu mode ON 😌

Made my sleepover-famous secret Maggi using my steamer setup… and before you judge —
YES, I ADD MILK TO MY MAGGI.

Everyone who’s tried it? NEVER complained 👀
So here’s me not gatekeeping anymore 👇

✨ MY SECRET MAGGI RECIPE ✨
• 1 glass water
• 1 pack Maggi noodles + 2 masala sachets
• A tea spoon of chilli powder
• A tea spoon of coriander powder
• A pinch of haldi
• Salt (as per taste)
• ½ tsp ghee
• A splash of milk (THE SECRET 🤫 for that creamy, almost cheesy texture)

➡️ Boil, mix, let it get slightly creamy — DONE.

Also steamed veggies on top because we pretend to be healthy 🥦✨

Gas saved. Mom happy. Me happier.
Now be honest —
MILK IN MAGGI: genius or crime? 👇

Students Protest in PSIT Kanpur !
PSIT कैंपस में दर्दनाक हादसा: जेसीबी से टकराकर BCA छात्र की मौत
कानपुर नगर के सचेंडी थाना क्षेत्र स्थित PSIT कॉलेज कैंपस में निर्माण कार्य के दौरान एक बड़ा हादसा हो गया। BCA फाइनल ईयर के 21 वर्षीय छात्र प्रखर सिंह की जेसीबी से टकराने के बाद मौत हो गई।

जानकारी के अनुसार, सोमवार शाम करीब 5 बजे छुट्टी के बाद प्रखर बाइक से कॉलेज के पीछे वाले गेट से बाहर निकल रहे थे। इसी दौरान एक तेज रफ्तार कार से उड़ी धूल के कारण आगे खड़ी जेसीबी उन्हें दिखाई नहीं दी और उनकी बाइक सीधी उससे टकरा गई।

गंभीर हालत में पहले उन्हें कल्याणपुर स्थित BMC अस्पताल ले जाया गया, जहां से रेफर कर सर्वोदय नगर के रीजेंसी अस्पताल भेजा गया। देर रात इलाज के दौरान डॉक्टरों ने उन्हें मृत घोषित कर दिया।

परिजनों ने आरोप लगाया है कि कॉलेज परिसर में बिना पर्याप्त सुरक्षा इंतजाम के निर्माण कार्य कराया जा रहा था। पुलिस मामले की जांच कर रही है।
